sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution authorizing the City Manager to amend Contract No. 31900143 and
any necessary future amendments with Passport Labs Inc. to provide a Parking
Management System for the Police Department, extending the term by another 18
months to August 4, 2027 and increasing this contract by $2,000,000 for a total not to
exceed $7,365,545.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
In 2019, City Council adopted Resolution No. 68,633-N.S. authorizing a five-year
contract with Passport Labs Inc. for a total not to exceed $2.87 million. This contract is
associated with a contra revenue account that absorbs the citation processing, permit,
and collection fees to support the contract. The projected fees to be absorbed by the
contra revenue account over the duration of the contract was $5,365,545. These fees
fluctuate depending on the number of citations and permits that are issued, payments
received, and delinquent citations being sought. The additional contract duration and
dollar amounts will allow a seamless transition to a follow-on contract.
sections.current_situation:
The City’s contract with Passport Labs Inc. for parking citation management was set to
expire on February 4, 2024. On January 30, 2024, Council approved a two-year
contract extension and additional appropriation for the performance of this contract for a
total not to exceed $5,365,545. In order to complete a new Parking Management
System Request for Proposal (RFP), review and award, additional appropriations of the
existing contract are necessary. The previous RFP required almost 12 months to bid,
review, and award. Implementation may require six additional months to acquire
necessary equipment and software set-up in the City of Berkeley Finance system to
ensure robust reporting and multiple systems integration. This will also allow for
additional time to ramp up as well as maintain the existing contract to allow for overlap
of services when and if, a transition requires months of system validations.

Contract No. 31900143 Amendment: Passport Labs Inc. for a Parking Management System Consent CALENDAR
January 21, 2025
sections.background:
The Parking Management System RFP will require the development of contract
specifications developed by the City of Berkeley, key stakeholders from Customer
Service, Finance, Police, Public Works, and the Information Technology Departments.
In order to allow sufficient time to bid and award a new contract, additional
appropriations of the existing contract are necessary.
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
The current Parking Management System from Passport Labs Inc., allows the ability for
customers to purchase permits and make payments online which may result in reduced
Green House Gas emissions from customers and residents. The PMS also utilizes
electronic records, which may result in the reduction of paper and other resources.
sections.rationale:
Without this contract, the City of Berkeley would not be able to continue the issuance,
processing, and collection of parking violations occurring within the City of Berkeley.
This would also include the revenue generated for the City of Berkeley staff in various
facets of the parking program.
The Passport Labs Inc. contract provides citation processing, collections services, and
various customer service resources to support the City Parking Management System
and the associated revenue to the City of Berkeley. This contract also assists in
support of council directives to implement parking and enforcement programs in support
of the expanded Citywide Residential Preferential Parking (RPP) Program, football and
special event parking, and citation payment programs.
Passport’s Enforcement and Permitting platform allows the City of Berkeley staff to use
mobile handheld ticketing processes, online citation payments, digital permitting, end-to-
end citation processing and collections, and the industry’s most data-driven backend
management software.
sections.alternatives:
Taking no action would end the contract with Passport before a new RFP is issued,
developed, and implemented, thus ending the City of Berkeley’s ability to support the
Parking Management Program and capture the revenue generated to support parking
operations, including staff costs reliant upon this revenue stream.
CONTACT PERSON
Jennifer Louis, Chief of Police, 510-981-5700
sections.attachments:
1: Resolution
Page 2
Page 3 of 3
RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
Contract No. 31900143 Amendment: Passport Labs Incorporated for a Parking
Management System
WHEREAS, the City of Berkeley executed contract No. 31900143 to Passport Labs
Incorporated for a Parking Management System beginning February 4, 2019 expiring on
February 4, 2024; and
WHEREAS, the original contract was authorized by the City Council of the City of
Berkeley under resolution #68,633-N.S. for five years and not to exceed $2,870,000
contract; and
WHEREAS, on January 30, 2024 the City Council of the City of Berkeley approved a
two-year contract extension and additional appropriations not to exceed $5,365, 545;
and
WHEREAS, this contract is associated with a contra revenue account that absorbs the
citation processing, permit and collection fees to support this contract; and
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that the
City Manager is authorized to execute a contract amendment to Contract No. 31900143
and any necessary future amendments with Passport Labs Incorporated to extend the
term by another 18 months to August 4, 2027 and increase this contract by $2,000,000
for a total not to exceed $7,365,545.
